description: This figure presents the finite element simulation setups for the low-density high-modulus TiB₂ reinforced Fe-Mn-Al-Ti-B steel, which consists of austenite, δ-ferrite, and TiB₂ phases. Subplot (a) shows the EBSD-based 200 μm × 200 μm representative volume element (RVE) with periodic boundary conditions, marking up/down/left/right edges, reference point RP-1 for loading and RP-2 for fixation. Subplot (b) displays the 2D finite element model distinguishing different phase layers: γ-Fe, γ-Fe/TiB₂ phase boundary, TiB₂, δ-Fe/TiB₂ phase boundary, and δ-Fe, with the color legend: blue for austenite, red for δ-ferrite, yellow for TiB₂. Subplot (c) provides a close-up of local finite element meshes, which adopts a 0.4 μm mesh size corresponding to 500×500 elements to improve simulation accuracy. This RVE setup is designed to correlate the microstructure and mechanical properties of the studied steel, and its validity is confirmed by matching simulation results with experimental mechanical responses.
